pattern: An in-order walk of a binary search tree visits values in ascending order, so the smallest gap between any two nodes is the smallest gap between adjacent values in that sorted stream.
hints: The closest pair of values in a search tree are not always parent and child; think about what order the values come out in when you traverse it the right way.
An in-order traversal of a BST yields the values sorted, so the answer is the minimum difference between consecutive values in that sequence.
Walk in-order while remembering only the previously visited value; each step, update the running minimum with the current value minus the previous one.

edge_inputs.description: small balanced tree where the minimum gap is one
closest pair are not adjacent in the tree structure
gap of one buried deep on opposite branches

stress.description: random BST built by inserting a shuffled permutation of distinct values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(2, max(2, size))
    vals = rng.sample(range(0, 100001), n)
    order = vals[:]
    rng.shuffle(order)
    # build BST as nested dict then serialize to BFS level-order with nulls
    root = None
    nodes = {}
    def insert(v):
        nonlocal root
        if root is None:
            root = v
            nodes[v] = [None, None]
            return
        cur = root
        while True:
            l, r = nodes[cur]
            if v < cur:
                if l is None:
                    nodes[cur][0] = v
                    nodes[v] = [None, None]
                    return
                cur = l
            else:
                if r is None:
                    nodes[cur][1] = v
                    nodes[v] = [None, None]
                    return
                cur = r
    for v in order:
        insert(v)
    out = []
    from collections import deque
    q = deque([root])
    while q:
        cur = q.popleft()
        if cur is None:
            out.append(None)
            continue
        out.append(cur)
        l, r = nodes[cur]
        q.append(l)
        q.append(r)
    while out and out[-1] is None:
        out.pop()
    return [out]
stress.note: constructing via insertion keeps the array a valid BST level-order form
